text: Build the hackathon extension: visible aim-to-commit provenance with dependency context
description: Create one coherent, judge-visible capability after 13 July: from an aim, show implementation evidence from Git commits (existing realized-commit signal), the aim's supporting dependencies, and linked-repo context. The experience should answer: what did we intend, what did it depend on, and what code actually realized it? Reuse existing .bowman persistence, linked-repo model, and commit-reference machinery. Keep scope to a polished vertical slice with tests; do not attempt a platform rewrite.
reflection: Implemented visible aim-to-commit provenance: backend Git log query keyed by exact aim UUID, Implementation evidence in AimEditModal beside dependency/repo context, and focused tests. Verified: backend test 1/1, AimEditModal tests 23/23, backend TypeScript build, frontend Vite production build, git diff --check. Full frontend type-check remains blocked by pre-existing TypeScript 6 baseUrl deprecation in tsconfig.

reflections.context: Runtime-independent validation against real repository history exposed that Aimparency commits conventionally cite 8-character aim UUID prefixes, not necessarily full UUIDs.
reflections.outcome: Aligned the Git evidence query with MCP reconciliation by searching the stable 8-character prefix. Verified against aim 0733cb72-c746-478c-b06e-ae4faa2af712: the query returned real commit 93fdf3e with author, timestamp, and subject.
reflections.effectiveness: The feature now works with established project history and remains compatible with full UUID references because those include the prefix. Backend tests pass 2/2 and TypeScript build passes.
reflections.lesson: End-to-end evidence must be tested against repository conventions, not only synthetic parser fixtures.

status.comment: Implementation and internal verification complete; awaiting human review per repository policy.
